text.title: RAG Evaluation Metrics
text.summary: Retrieval metrics (Precision@K, Recall@K, MRR, NDCG, Hit Rate) and generation metrics (Faithfulness, Answer Relevance) with production targets.

section.title: Retrieval Metrics

rule.statement: Require stable document IDs before computing retrieval metrics; if your vector DB returns raw chunks, compute IDs as hash(content).
rule.rationale: Precision@K and Recall@K require matching retrieved doc IDs against relevance labels. Without IDs the calculation is impossible.

list.item: NDCG@K: graded relevance with position weighting — target > 0.8
Hit Rate: 1 if any relevant doc in top-K else 0 — target > 0.9

section.p: Position matters: relevant docs at position 10+ are often ignored by the LLM due to context window positional bias.

rule.statement: Do not conflate RAGAS Faithfulness (grounded in retrieved context) with factual accuracy (correct against world knowledge) — a faithful answer can still be factually wrong if the retrieved context is wrong.
rule.rationale: These measure different failure modes that require different fixes: bad retrieval vs hallucinating generation.

list.item: Faithfulness: claims supported by context / total claims — target > 0.85
Answer Relevance: does the answer address the question? — target > 0.9
Context Relevance: is retrieved context relevant to the question? — target > 0.7
Hallucination Rate: unsupported or fabricated claims — target < 0.1
Citation Coverage: claims backed by source references — target > 0.8
RAGAS Score: Faithfulness + Answer Relevance + Context Relevance combined
Answer Correctness: semantic similarity + factual overlap against ground truth
Context Precision: relevant chunks / total retrieved chunks — proxy for retrieval efficiency
Have relevance labels: use Precision@K, Recall@K, NDCG, MRR
No ground truth: use Faithfulness, Answer Relevance (RAGAS LLM-based)
Production monitoring: Latency + Faithfulness + Hit Rate (lightweight)
Full model comparison: complete RAGAS suite + retrieval metrics
